proof_trace: Debug assert validates NULL input but is disabled in release builds (NDEBUG), allowing NULL pointer dereference in loop when AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet fails. No runtime guard ensures safety.
attack_scenario: User triggers memory exhaustion causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet to return NULL, leading to DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet(NULL) which crashes on dereference.
reason: Matches RA-N2-N1 — DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet NULL from failed Acquire

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet returns NULL on memory allocation failure. AddNoiseImage does not check the return value before dereferencing random_info[id] at line 377 (and line 326 if OPENMP enabled), causing NULL pointer dereference.
attack_scenario: Attacker causes memory exhaustion leading to allocation failure, triggering NULL dereference in AddNoiseImage and denial-of-service.
reason: Matches RA-N2-O7 — AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet NULL in AddNoiseImage

proof_trace: After `_multiCall(calls)` executes at L109, there is no mechanism to reset token allowances granted to addresses other than `s_auction`. The only post-multiCall approval action (L111) sets `assetOut` allowance for `msg.sender` (the auction), leaving any approvals to arbitrary spenders set during `_multiCall` persistent. This allows `AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE` to include `token.approve(attacker, MAX_UINT)` calls in the solution, creating a persistent allowance that bypasses the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` restriction on `withdraw()`.
attack_scenario: `AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE` includes `Call(token, abi.encodeWithSelector(IERC20.approve.selector, attacker, type(uint256).max))` in the `solution` passed to `bid()`; after `auctionCallback` completes the approval persists, allowing the attacker to call `token.transferFrom(AuctionBidder, attacker, balance)` and drain the contract's holdings in a follow-up transaction.
reason: Cross-project data contamination — AuctionBidder.sol unrelated to target

proof_trace: ExpandMirrorKernelInfo calls CloneKernelInfo which can return NULL on memory allocation failure, but fails to check the return value before passing it to RotateKernelInfo, which will dereference the NULL pointer.
attack_scenario: A user provides a kernel with the '<' expansion flag, and under low memory conditions CloneKernelInfo returns NULL, causing RotateKernelInfo to dereference NULL and crash.
reason: Matches RA-N2-O2/O3/O4 — CloneKernelInfo NULL in ExpandMirrorKernelInfo
